roadmap.note: sha256 re-verified byte-identical to the Phase 0 checkpoint's recorded hash (no drift since Phase 0); the Phase 0 checkpoint's own 'lines' field (287) was a stale/incorrect measurement pre-dating this phase and is corrected here to the actual wc -l count (384), not evidence of file drift.

defects_found_and_fixed.defect: Used allowedMcpServers (managed-scope-only key, wrong shape) instead of the project-scope key enabledMcpjsonServers to auto-approve .mcp.json servers.
tools: frontmatter used lowercase/invalid tool names ([read, grep, git, shell, ...]); git/shell/github are not real Claude Code tool identifiers.
defects_found_and_fixed.evidence: Contrasted against working tools: Read, Grep, Bash/Edit in implementation-agent.md and pr-reviewer.md.
defects_found_and_fixed.fix: Corrected both to tools: Read, Grep, Bash.

defects_found_and_fixed.defect: No explicit skill-promotion policy; 'repeated procedures split into skills' criterion had no governing rule, only incidental skill count.
defects_found_and_fixed.fix: Added a promotion rule within the file's existing 20-line budget.

live_behavior_evidence: PreToolUse block path: Edit on .env.production and Bash rm under migrations/ both exit 2 with stderr reason, tested with a Windows-native cwd payload.
PreToolUse allow path: Read on README.md exits 0 and is logged by the Phase 0 harness.
PreToolUse error path: malformed/empty stdin exits 1, a documented non-blocking-but-visible error per the hooks doc's exit-code contract.
Agent-tool delegation: pr-reviewer spawned via the Agent tool, called Bash and Read, returned real live git status output (tool_uses: 2).

reflect-agent spawned three times and enforced its own frontmatter-defined rules when declining out-of-role probes, confirming role-definition loading independent of the specific tool-use test outcome.
exit_criteria: The repo has a clear project CLAUDE.md and .claude/rules/ layout; nothing depends on ~/.claude/.
Repeated procedures are split into project skills instead of duplicated in chat or root docs.
Required external tools are available through approved MCP servers declared in .mcp.json.
Unsafe or out-of-policy tool calls are blocked or annotated by project-scoped hooks before the next loop step.
Delegated subagent work uses the Agent tool pattern with role definitions in .claude/agents/.
remaining_risks: reflect-agent's own tool execution under the adversarial-shaped prompts used here remains unconfirmed; its refusals are role-correct behavior, not a defect, but the tools: fix for that file was cross-verified via format parity with pr-reviewer rather than a clean live tool call from reflect-agent itself.
The Phase 0 harness remains a project-local surrogate for Claude Code's native transcript store, unchanged in this phase.
Phases 2-7 remain incomplete and are not implicitly validated by these Phase 1 fixes.
next_goal: Phase 1 is complete. Leave Phase 2 untouched until explicitly directed.
